din125i.source: variant key sharing DIN 125 geometry; the app already serves it the base drawing, so this aliases the base.

din126i.source: variant key sharing DIN 126 geometry; the app already serves it the base drawing, so this aliases the base.

din127.source: DIN 127-B:1997 (M12; d1 12.2, d2 21.1, cross-section width b 4.0 x thickness s 2.5). Was wrongly d2 20.2 / section 3.5. section is the axial thickness (2.5); the radial width follows from d2-d1. Aspen/Fasteners.eu.

din127p.source: plated (electro-zinc) variant of DIN 127; plating does not change geometry, so this aliases the base drawing.

din128.source: DIN 128-A:2011 (M12, curved; d1 12.2, d2 21.1, material thickness s 2.1, free height ~3.35). Was wrongly d2 21.0 / s 1.5 (1.5 is not a DIN 128 M12 value). Aspen/Fasteners.eu.

din128a.source: DIN 128 Form A is the base curved spring washer; this key aliases it.

din137a.source: DIN 137 A (M12, curved/domed spring washer, gewölbt; d1=13, d2=24, s=1.2, continuous ring with no split). Reuses curved_washer with gap_deg=0. Dome height representative (free height ~3 mm; DIN envelope 2.5-5 mm) — the cone tilt is chosen to sit in that range, not a published value.

din1440.source: DIN 1440 (pin Ø12 / M12, medium washer for bolts and pins; d1=12, d2=25, s=3). Dimensions from Fasteners.eu.

din1440i.source: variant key sharing DIN 1440 geometry; the app already serves it the base drawing, so this aliases the base.

din1587.source: DIN 1587 hexagon domed cap (acorn) nut (M12): s=18 (current ISO 272-aligned width; legacy pre-1992 s=19 not shipped), total height m=22, hex body 10 -> dome_height=12 (derived, not directly tabulated). Confirmed vs Westfield Fasteners + Andrews Fasteners + fasteners.eu DIN 1587 tables.

din1816.source: DIN 1816 cross-hole round nut (Kreuzlochmutter), M12x1.5 fine thread. d=28.0 and h=6.0 tabulated by theo-schrauben.de + seefelder.net + online-schrauben.de + mcmaster.com. n_holes=4 tabulated by theo-schrauben.de + seefelder.net. hole_d=3.0 (d4) tabulated by theo-schrauben.de; online-schrauben.de confirms M3. d3=23.0 (pitch circle) tabulated by theo-schrauben.de + online-schrauben.de + seefelder.net. hole_depth=4.0 derived: OD_radius(14)−(d3/2−hole_d/2)=14−10=4 mm; hole floor at r=10 mm, bore wall at r≈5.2 mm, no intersection. bore=10.4 from ISO 724 D1=12−1.082532×1.5=10.376 mm. Holes are 4 radial bores perpendicular to the nut axis drilled into the OD wall (tommy-bar spanner), NOT axial holes in the top face. Holes placed at mid-height (no axial offset dimensioned in any public table). The bold concentric ring in the DIN 1816 face-view drawing is the standard internal-thread representation (the same ring appears on the DIN 546 drawing, which has no step); it is not modelled (threads are envelope-only). Any 0.5 mm top-face Planfläche (z=0.5 mm at d3=23 mm) is a sub-visible chamfer-scale detail below the family's fine-feature threshold and is not modelled.

din2093.source: DIN 2093 (disc spring / Belleville, representative; nominal, unverified)

din2093d.source: variant key for the DIN 2093 disc spring; identical geometry, aliased to the base.

din315.source: DIN 315 wing nut, German Form D 'rounded wings' (M12), current standard (DIN 315:2016-12; no ISO equivalent). Envelope confirmed vs fasteners.eu + Fuller Fasteners + schrauben-lexikon + ITA Fasteners + globalfastener DIN 315-D tables: overall height h=33.5 (max), boss/hub base dia d2=23 (max), collar/top dia d3=19.5 (max, the tapered hub top), wing span e=65 (max, tip-to-tip), blade thickness g1=4.9 (max). bore=10.1 is the M12 thread minor (ISO 724, external to DIN 315's table). boss_h=14.0 is a representative hub height (the DIN 'm' symbol 10-14, read inconsistently across sources, taken as hub height) -> form, not asserted normative. The hub taper (d2->d3) and general form follow the DIN 315 geometry (as also implemented in the open-source FreeCAD Fasteners Workbench, LGPL); the exact wing radii are not published, so the rounded wing outline is representative form matched to the legacy DIN 315 drawing. Only the envelope is a sourced dimension.

din431.source: DIN 431 hexagon pipe nut, representative size G 3/8: s=27.0, m=8.0, bore=16.66 (G3/8 major dia per ISO 228-1). Confirmed vs Fasteners.eu + Fuller Fasteners DIN 431 tables.

din432.source: DIN 432 (M12, external nose lock washer; d2=30, s=1.2, nose width 4.5; nose length representative)

din432p.source: plated (electro-zinc) variant of DIN 432; plating does not change geometry, so this aliases the base drawing.

din433.source: DIN 433-1:2020 (M12, small OD; d1 13, d2 20, s 2.0). Thickness matches ISO 7092 (same washer); was wrongly 2.5 (the DIN 125 thickness). Fasteners.eu / Accu.

din433i.source: variant key sharing DIN 433 geometry; the app already serves it the base drawing, so this aliases the base.

din434.source: DIN 434 (M12, square taper washer for U-channel, 8% slope, 26x30, hole 13.5; thin edge e 3.7, mean h 4.9, thick edge 6.1). Dimensions from Fasteners.eu / Fuller Fasteners / Wegertseder tables.

din434p.source: plated (electro-zinc) variant of DIN 434; plating does not change geometry, so this aliases the base drawing.

din435.source: DIN 435 (M12, taper washer for I-beam flanges, 14% slope, 26x30, hole 13.5; thin edge 5.0, mean h 6.2, thick edge 7.4). Rectangular like DIN 434, not square. Dimensions from Fasteners.eu / Fuller Fasteners / Intafast tables.

din435p.source: plated (electro-zinc) variant of DIN 435; plating does not change geometry, so this aliases the base drawing.

din436p.source: plated (electro-zinc) variant of DIN 436; plating does not change geometry, so this aliases the base drawing.

din462.source: DIN 462 (M12, internal tab lock washer; d1=12, d2=28, s=0.8, tab 5x9.3, bent up at the bore)

din462p.source: plated (electro-zinc) variant of DIN 462; plating does not change geometry, so this aliases the base drawing.

din463.source: DIN 463 (M12, two-tab lock washer, long+short; d1 13, d2 24, s 1, tab width b 12, l1 28 / l2 15 from centre). Was d2 30 (the DIN 93/432 value) with tab width 10 (the M10 value). Tab lengths here are the projections beyond the OD (l1/l2 minus radius). Standard withdrawn. Westfield/Ferrobolt DIN 463.

din463p.source: plated (electro-zinc) variant of DIN 463; plating does not change geometry, so this aliases the base drawing.

din466.source: DIN 466 high knurled nut (M12): knurled head OD d=40, total height 25 = head h=15 + plain boss (collar_d=22, collar_h=10), tabulated and confirmed vs Boutet Technics DIN 466 table + Fastenright + JW Winco (Aspen/Fuller M10 row dk=36/ds=20 extrapolates cleanly). Smooth cylindrical envelope drawn (the knurl is a fine feature, not modelled, like the thread). bore=10.1 (M12 minor, ISO 724).

din467.source: DIN 467 flat/low knurled nut (M12): knurled head OD d=40 (d1) with a reduced-diameter plain boss collar_d=22 (d2) on the bearing face; total height 12 = knurled head h=10 (k) + boss collar_h=2. Tabulated and confirmed vs Aspen Fasteners DIN 467 spec (d1=40,d2=22,h=12,k=10) + Scribd DIN 467 table + Fastenright + JW Winco. Smooth cylindrical envelope drawn (the knurl is a fine feature, not modelled, like the thread). bore=10.1 (M12 minor, ISO 724).

din471.source: DIN 471 external circlip for shaft, normal type, d1=12mm. d_seat=d2=11.0mm (groove diameter, the ring's inner edge seats here; d2<d1). thickness=s=1.0mm. Radial section tapers from b=1.8mm at the back to a=2.4mm at the lug/ear ends (a>b: the pierced ears project outward past the body by a-b, the internal-thread-free stamped circlip form). lug_hole_d=d5=1.5mm. Confirmed vs DIN 471:2011 official text + RoyMech + fasten.it + Danish DIN 471 table (s=1.0, b=1.8, d2=11.0); a=2.4 from Beneri + ItaFasteners + ArconRing DIN 471 tables; d5=1.5 from Aspen Fasteners + fasten.it. gap_deg=20 and lug_project=0.5 are representative (not tabulated in public DIN 471 sources).

din472.source: DIN 472 internal circlip for bore, normal type, d1=12mm. d_seat=d2=12.5mm (bore groove diameter, the ring's outer edge seats here; d2>d1). thickness=s=1.0mm. Radial section tapers from b=1.7mm at the back to a=3.0mm at the lug/ear ends (a>b, same taper direction as DIN 471). lug_hole_d=d5=1.5mm. Confirmed vs Westfield Fasteners DIN 472 table + fasten.it + ItaFasteners + AMESweb (s=1.0, b=1.7, a=3.0, d5=1.5); d2=12.5 from Westfield + fasten.it + engdatabase.com. gap_deg=25 and lug_project=0.5 are representative (not tabulated in public DIN 472 sources).

din508.source: DIN 508 T-slot nut (Nutenstein), M12x1.75 coarse thread, 14 mm T-slot nominal (DIN 650). foot_w=22.0 (DIN symbol e, foot base across-slot width, sits in DIN 650 ~23.5 mm undercut) confirmed by Hoffmann Group + McMaster-Carr + Dönges/AMF + SFS tables. neck_w=13.7 (DIN symbol a, narrow neck that passes through the 14 mm slot opening) confirmed by Hoffmann Group + Fuller Fasteners + Halder USA + JW Winco + fasten.it + Dönges/AMF — nominal is 14 mm (slot designation), DIN tolerance gives a_max=13.7 mm; any appearance of '16' as a width in online tables is total height h, not neck width. length=22.0 (DIN symbol s, block length along slot direction) confirmed by Fasten.it + Fuller + Hoffmann/AMF + Dönges/AMF + SFS + McMaster + JW Winco. height=16.0 (h) confirmed by same sources. foot_h=8.0 derived as h−k (k=8 tabulated). bore=10.1 = M12x1.75 coarse minor diameter per ISO 724 (D1=12−1.082532x1.75=10.106 mm). chamfer=1.0 mm representative: DIN 508 does not tabulate a chamfer; 0.5–1.0 mm x45 deg is standard shop practice for M12 (per Fuller and Hoffmann references); not a standardized DIN feature.

din546.source: DIN 546 slotted round nut (Schlitzmutter), M12x1.75 coarse thread. d=21.0 and h=10.0 tabulated by fasteners.eu + definitemetal.com + torqbolt.com + westfieldfasteners.co.uk. slot_w=4.0 (n nominal) tabulated by definitemetal.com. slot_depth=3.8 is t_min per definitemetal.com (DIN 546 table); vital-parts.co.uk manufactured example gives 4.3 mm (within tolerance). bore=10.1 from ISO 724 D1=12−1.082532×1.75=10.106 mm. Single diametral face slot (partial depth, one slot across the top face, not full-height). All fields tabulated ≥2 independent sources except slot_w and slot_depth (single table; representative values).

din557.source: DIN 557 square nut, product grade C (M12): s=18 (current ISO 272 / SW18; obsolescent pre-1994 s=19 not shipped), m=10 (max/nominal), bore=10.1 (M12 minor dia). Top face chamfered (chamfer-circle diameter = s, a drawing form requirement, not tabulated -> representative), bearing face flat. Confirmed vs fasteners.eu + Fuller Fasteners + TC Fixings + Aspen Fasteners DIN 557 tables.

din6319c.source: DIN 6319 Form C (M12, Kugelscheibe / spherical washer; d1=13, d3=24, h2=4.6, flat top with convex spherical underside). Was h2=4.0 (the M10 value). Mating sphere radius representative (~26 mm): DIN tables publish it inconsistently, chosen to seat in the Form D/G cup. Aspen/Halder DIN 6319.

din6319d.source: DIN 6319 Form D (M12, Kegelpfanne / conical seat; d2=14.2, d4=24, h3=5.0, flat bottom with concave spherical recess receiving the Form C washer). Was d2=13 / h3=3: the seat bore (14.2) is larger than the Form C washer bore (13.0), and h3 is 5.0 not 3.0. Mating sphere radius representative (~26 mm), same as Form C. Aspen/Halder/Growermetal DIN 6319.

din6330.source: DIN 6330 hexagon nut height 1.5d (version B), M12: s=18.0, m=18.0 (=1.5x12). Confirmed vs Fasteners.eu DIN 6330 table + Norelem/Kipp DIN 6330 datasheets.

din6331.source: DIN 6331 hexagon nut with collar, height 1.5d (M12): s=18 (current ISO 272 / SW18), m=18 (=1.5x12, total height incl. collar), collar diameter d1/dc=25, collar height a=4, bore=10.1 (M12 minor dia). Collar dc=25 exceeds the hex across-corners (20.78), a visible bearing ring; plain cylindrical (no edge chamfer). Confirmed vs fasteners.eu + Aspen Fasteners + Hommel-Hercules + NF GAB + Wurth DIN 6331 tables.

din6334.source: DIN 6334 hexagon coupling nut (Verbindungsmutter / connector nut), M12x1.75 coarse: across-flats s=19.0 (SW19 is the confirmed current value for DIN 6334, not 18), length m=l=36.0 (=3x12), bore=10.1 (M12 coarse minor). Plain hexagonal prism with double-chamfered ends, through-threaded (joins two threaded rods) - same shape as a hex nut, just tall. Confirmed vs Fasteners.eu + Fuller Fasteners + Andrews Fasteners + Bolt Depot DIN 6334 tables.

din6340.source: DIN 6340 (M12, washer for clamping fixtures / Scheiben für Spannzeuge, heavy; d1=13, d2=35, s=5). Dimensions from Fasteners.eu / Ganter tables.

din6340d.source: variant key for DIN 6340; identical geometry, aliased to the base.

din6796.source: DIN 6796 (M12 conical spring lock washer; d1 13, d2 29, material thickness s 3.0, free height ~3.95). Was s 3.2. cone_angle representative. Fuller/Monroe/DIN 6796.

din6797a.source: DIN 6797 A (M12, external tooth / Zahnscheibe, 10 teeth)

din6797j.source: DIN 6797 J (M12, internal tooth / Innenzahn, 10 teeth)

din6798a.source: DIN 6798 A (M12, external serrated / Fächerscheibe; d1 13, d2 20.5, s 1.0, min 16 teeth). Was d2 20.0. Fasteners.eu / Aspen.

din6798d.source: catalog 'Form D' label is not a real DIN 6798 form; its geometry is Form V, so this aliases din6798v.

din6798j.source: DIN 6798 J (M12, internal serrated / Fächerscheibe; d1 13, d2 20.5, s 1.0, min 16 teeth). Shares the A-form d1/d2/s series. Was d2 21.0 / s 1.3. Fasteners.eu / Aspen.

din6798v.source: DIN 6798 V (M12, countersunk external serrated / Senkzahn; d1 13, d3 23.0, s2 0.5, min 26 teeth, countersunk head). Was d3 22 / s 0.8 / 18 teeth (the M8 row). cone_angle representative. Fasteners.eu / Ferrobolt.

din6916i.source: variant key sharing DIN 6916 geometry; the app already serves it the base drawing, so this aliases the base.

din6923.source: DIN 6923 hex flange nut (M12): s=18, m=12, d_flange=26, flange_thickness(c)=1.8, bore=10.1 (M12 minor dia). Confirmed vs Fasteners.eu + Fuller Fasteners DIN 6923 tables.

din6925.source: DIN 6925 all-metal high prevailing-torque hexagon nut (M12, ISO 7042 counterpart): s=18 (SW18, ISO 4033 base, not DIN 934) and total height m=12.0 confirmed by three independent vendor tables (nan-long, fastenersco, zhenkunmach) and fasteners.eu. Kept distinct from ISO 7042 (h_max 13.3, 2012 edition) because the confirmed DIN 6925 envelope differs. The deformed metal crown is drawn as a representative truncated cone (top_h/top_d/top_d2 representative form). bore=10.1 (M12 minor, ISO 724).

din6926.source: DIN 6926 prevailing-torque nylon-insert hex flange nut (M12): metal envelope (s=18, m=12, d_flange=26, c=1.8) identical to DIN 6923; the nylon crown (total height 16.1) sits above the metal body and is not modelled, so this aliases the DIN 6923 drawing. Confirmed vs Fasteners.eu + Fuller Fasteners DIN 6926.

din6927.source: DIN 6927 prevailing-torque all-metal hex flange nut (M12): s/d_flange/c identical to DIN 6923; total metal envelope height m=13.8 (all-metal locking crown adds ~1.8mm). The locking deformation is not modelled (drawn as a smooth flange nut of the true envelope height). Confirmed vs Fasteners.eu + bwindustrialsales DIN 6927.

din70852.source: DIN 70852 groove nut (M12x1.5 fine thread): OD d=22, height h=6, bore=10.4 (M12x1.5 minor dia), n_slots=4, slot width slot_w=4.5, slot radial depth slot_depth=2 (slot-floor dia D3=18), slot axial groove depth slot_h=c=1.8 (partial depth, opens from one face, not full height). Confirmed vs Kipp + Norelem + Maedler + ferrofast + Wegertseder tables.

din7349.source: DIN 7349 (M12, thick washer for clamping sleeves)

din7349d.source: DIN 7349 (M12, heavy thick washer for bolts with heavy clamping sleeves; d1=13, d2=30, s=6). Dimensions from Fasteners.eu.

din7603d.source: DIN 7603 (12x18, sealing ring / Dichtring, copper/aluminium; d1=12, d2=18). Thickness representative (form/material dependent, commonly 1.0-1.5 mm; 1.5 chosen).

din7980.source: DIN 7980 (M12, spring lock washer for cylinder-head screws; d1=12.2, d2=18, section 2.5, split ring)

din7980p.source: plated (electro-zinc) variant of DIN 7980; plating does not change geometry, so this aliases the base drawing.

din80705.source: DIN 80705 thin hexagon nut with reduced width across flats, M12x1.5: s=17.0, m=6.0. Confirmed vs Fasteners.eu + din-fasteners.com + Ferrobend DIN 80705 tables.

din9021i.source: variant key sharing DIN 9021 geometry; the app already serves it the base drawing, so this aliases the base.

din917.source: DIN 917 hexagon cap nut, low type (M12): s=18, total height m=16, hex body 8 (m' minimum) -> dome_height=8 (derived). Confirmed vs fasteners.eu + Fuller Fasteners + TorqBolt DIN 917 tables.

din928.source: DIN 928 square weld nut, product grade A (M12): s=19, m=9.5 (nut body height), bore=10.1 (M12 minor dia). Plain flat square prism, no top chamfer; the three resistance-weld projections on the bearing face are consumed during welding and are not modelled (don't-fabricate). Confirmed vs fasteners.eu + Fuller Fasteners + fasten.it + McMaster-Carr DIN 928 tables.

din93.source: DIN 93 (M12, one-tab lock washer; d2=30, s=1, tab width 12; tab length representative)

din935.source: DIN 935 hexagon slotted / castle nut (M12): s=18 (current ISO 272 / SW18; legacy SW19 not shipped), m=15, un-slotted height w/m1=10, crown dk=16, 6 slots, slot width n/e=3.5 (min; max 3.8), bore=10.1 (M12 minor dia). Crown modelled fully slotted (no solid crown band below the slots; hex/crown transition not tabulated). Confirmed vs Fuller Fasteners + Aspen Fasteners + Trojan SF + Fabory DIN 935 tables.

din936.source: DIN 936 hexagon thin/jam nut, M12: s=19.0, m=7.0. Distinct from ISO 4035 (wider flats, 1mm taller). Confirmed vs Fasteners.eu + Fuller Fasteners DIN 936 tables.

din937.source: DIN 937 hexagon thin (low) slotted / castle nut (M12): s=19 (SW19, the withdrawn legacy standard's actual across-flats), m=10, m1=6.0 (w slot-floor height, not the 5.3 m' proof-load minimum), crown dk=17, 6 slots, slot width 3.5, bore=10.1. Crown modelled fully slotted. Confirmed vs fasteners.eu + Fuller Fasteners + fasenco + Aspen Fasteners DIN 937 tables.

din93p.source: plated (electro-zinc) variant of DIN 93; plating does not change geometry, so this aliases the base drawing.

din979.source: DIN 979 hexagon thin slotted / castle nut (M12, current successor to DIN 937): s=18 (ISO 272 / SW18), m=10, m1=5, crown dk=16 (Aspen's 17 is a mixed-edition outlier), 6 slots, slot width 3.5, bore=10.1. Crown modelled fully slotted. Confirmed vs Fuller Fasteners + Trojan SF DIN 979 tables.

din981.source: DIN 981 rolling-bearing locknut (KM1, M12x1 fine thread): OD d=22, height h=4, bore=10.9 (M12x1 minor dia), n_slots=4, slot width slot_w=3, slot radial depth slot_depth=2 (slot-floor dia 18), slot axial depth slot_h=t=2 (the grooves open from one face, partial depth, not full height). Confirmed vs SKF + MISUMI/FAG + HC-Maschinentechnik + Wegertseder tables. The internal keyway (for the MB tab washer) is not modelled.

din986.source: DIN 986 all-metal prevailing-torque hexagon domed cap nut (M12): s=18 (ISO 4032-aligned SW18; legacy SW19 not shipped), total height h2=22.5, hex body h1=13.5 -> dome_height=9. The prevailing-torque crown deformation is not modelled (envelope-only, same precedent as DIN 6927). Confirmed vs Fabory + Bossard BN 167 + Fuller Fasteners + fasteners.eu.

din988d.source: DIN 988 (12x18, shim ring / Passscheibe; d1=12, d2=18). Thickness representative (DIN 988 is a shim series 0.1-2 mm; a mid 1.0 mm ring is drawn).

iso10511.source: ISO 10511 prevailing-torque thin hexagon nut with non-metallic (nylon) insert (M12): s=18 (SW18, ISO 4035 base) and total height m=10.2 (h_max 10.23) tabulated from the ISO 10511:2012 Table 1 PDF, cross-checked against fasteners.eu. Metal envelope only (nylon ring not drawn). top_h/top_d are representative form (the metal skirt profile is not tabulated). bore=10.1 (M12 minor, ISO 724).

iso10512.source: ISO 10512 is the fine-pitch (M12x1.5) counterpart of ISO 7040 with an identical M12 metal envelope (s=18, m=14.9 per the ISO 10512:2012 Table 1 PDF); pitch is invisible at label scale, so it aliases the ISO 7040 drawing (precedent iso8673/8674/8675).

iso10513.source: ISO 10513 is the fine-pitch (M12x1.5) all-metal high counterpart of ISO 7042 with an identical 2012 M12 metal envelope (s=18, m=13.3); pitch is invisible at label scale, so it aliases the ISO 7042 drawing.

iso21670.source: ISO 21670 hex weld nut with flange (M12): s=18, m=15, d_flange=30.5, flange_thickness(c)=1.2 (rim 1.1-1.3 range), bore=10.1. Distinct from DIN 6923 (wider flange, thinner rim, taller). Weld projections on the bearing face are sub-visible and not modelled. Confirmed vs fasenco.com + globalfastener.com (ISO 21670:2003/2014) + Wurth.

iso4032.source: ISO 4032 hexagon regular nut (style 1), M12: s=18.0, m=10.8. Confirmed vs Fasteners.eu + wermac.org ISO 4032 dimension tables.

iso4033.source: ISO 4033 hexagon high nut (style 2), M12: s=18.0, m=12.0 (m=1.0d). Confirmed vs TorqBolt + fasten.it ISO 4033 tables.

iso4035.source: ISO 4035 hexagon thin nut (style 0), M12: s=18.0 (ISO value; the 19mm seen in some catalogues is the DIN 439B deviation), m=6.0. Confirmed vs Fuller Fasteners ISO 4035 spec + BS EN ISO 4035 table (Engineers Edge).

iso7035.source: ISO 7035 hexagon slotted nut, style 1 (M12): the ISO counterpart of DIN 935 with an identical M12 envelope (s=18, m=15, w=10, dk=16, 6 slots, 3.5), so it aliases the DIN 935 drawing. Confirmed vs fasteners.eu ISO 7035 + TorqBolt ISO 7035 + Bolting Specialist + combined DIN 935/ISO 7035 product listings.

iso7040.source: ISO 7040 prevailing-torque regular hexagon nut with non-metallic (nylon) insert (M12): s=18 (SW18, ISO 4032 base) and total height m=14.9 (h_max) tabulated from the ISO 7040:2012 Table 1 PDF, cross-checked against Aspen Fasteners DIN 982 PDF and fasteners.eu. Note: vendor 'ISO 7040 = s19' pages substitute DIN 982 data; the ISO standard is SW18. Metal envelope only. top_h/top_d representative form. bore=10.1 (M12 minor, ISO 724).

iso7041.source: ISO 7041 prevailing-torque high (style 2) hexagon nut with non-metallic (nylon) insert (M12): s=18 (SW18, ISO 4033 base) and total height m=16.1 (h_max) tabulated from the ISO 7041:2012 Table 1 PDF, cross-checked against stainlessfix.com (s=18, h_max=16.1, exact match). Metal envelope only. top_h/top_d representative form. bore=10.1 (M12 minor, ISO 724).

iso7042.source: ISO 7042 prevailing-torque all-metal high hexagon nut (M12): s=18 (SW18, ISO 4033 base) and total height m=13.3 (h_max) tabulated from the ISO 7042:2012 Table 1 PDF, cross-checked against globalfastener. The deformed metal crown is drawn as a representative truncated cone (top_h/top_d/top_d2 representative form). bore=10.1 (M12 minor, ISO 724).

iso7089p.source: plated (electro-zinc) variant of ISO 7089; plating does not change geometry, so this aliases the base drawing.

iso7091p.source: plated (electro-zinc) variant of ISO 7091; plating does not change geometry, so this aliases the base drawing.

iso7092p.source: plated (electro-zinc) variant of ISO 7092; plating does not change geometry, so this aliases the base drawing.

iso7093.source: ISO 7093-1 (M12, large series; d1 13, d2 37, s 3). Dimensionally identical to DIN 9021; was wrongly d2 30. Fasteners.eu.

iso7093p.source: plated (electro-zinc) variant of ISO 7093; plating does not change geometry, so this aliases the base drawing.

iso7094.source: ISO 7094 (M12, extra large series, grade C; d1 13.5, d2 44, s 4). Dimensionally equivalent to DIN 440 R; was wrongly holding the DIN 9021 values (13/37/3). Fasteners.eu / Keller-Kalmbach.

iso7094p.source: plated (electro-zinc) variant of ISO 7094; plating does not change geometry, so this aliases the base drawing.

iso7416p.source: plated (electro-zinc) variant of ISO 7416; plating does not change geometry, so this aliases the base drawing.

iso7719.source: ISO 7719 prevailing-torque all-metal regular hexagon nut (M12): s=18 (SW18, ISO 4032 base) and total height m=11.6 (h_max) tabulated from the ISO 7719:2012 Table 1 PDF, cross-checked against fasten.it. The deformed metal crown is drawn as a representative truncated cone (top_h/top_d/top_d2 representative form — the crimp profile is not tabulated). bore=10.1 (M12 minor, ISO 724).

iso7720.source: ISO 7720 is the property-class-9 variant of ISO 7042 (all-metal high), dimensionally identical for M12 (s=18, m=13.3 per ISO 7720:2012 / ISO 7042:2012 Table 1), so it aliases the ISO 7042 drawing. (The legacy dataset image for iso7720 was a nylon-insert drawing; ISO 7720 is all-metal, so the generated cone drawing is the correct form.)

iso8673.source: ISO 8673 = ISO 4032 regular hex nut with fine pitch; external body geometry identical to ISO 4032, aliased to that base drawing.

iso8674.source: ISO 8674 = ISO 4033 high hex nut with fine pitch; identical body, aliased to the base.

iso8675.source: ISO 8675 = ISO 4035 thin hex nut with fine pitch; identical body, aliased to the base.

iso8738.source: ISO 8738 (size 12, plain washer for clevis pins, dimensionally equivalent to DIN 1440; d1=12, d2=25, s=3).

iso8738p.source: plated (electro-zinc) variant of ISO 8738; plating does not change geometry, so this aliases the base drawing.
